prompt: I'm building the hero section of my SaaS landing page with Next.js 15 App Router, Tailwind v4, and shadcn/ui. The section has a headline + subheadline + a shadcn Button CTA ('Get started'), a stats row with three numbers ('1,234 users · 42% growth · 356 reviews') that should tick up from 0 when the section scrolls into view, and a grid of 6 feature cards below that stagger in on scroll. The whole thing should animate only the first time it enters the viewport, and respect prefers-reduced-motion. My app uses shadcn theme tokens throughout. Give me Hero.tsx as a single file. I've seen <AnimateNumber> referenced — is that the right tool here?
assertions: Uses shadcn theme tokens (text-foreground, bg-background, bg-primary, bg-card, border-border, text-muted-foreground, text-primary-foreground) — NOT concrete bg-white / bg-indigo-* / text-neutral-* utilities
Feature grid uses variants + stagger() from motion/react (not legacy staggerChildren number)
Feature grid uses whileInView with viewport={{ once: true }} so it doesn't re-animate on scroll back
Stat counter uses useMotionValue + animate() + useTransform rounding + motion component as child (<motion.span>{rounded}</motion.span>), NOT useState + setInterval and NOT toFixed-in-render
Explicitly declines <AnimateNumber> as a Motion+ paid component (not 'number-flow' or 'Motion-Plus examples' or similar misattribution)
Handles prefers-reduced-motion via useReducedMotion() or MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'
Uses shadcn Button for the CTA (imports from '@/components/ui/button' or equivalent, does not rebuild a button from scratch with motion.button and button-like classes)
Does not use any Motion+ components (Cursor, AnimateNumber, Ticker, Motion Studio MCP)
